title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers robots exclusion protocol, failure semantics, caching, and sitemaps into a changed RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin and use an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Robots Location Agent
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 7781 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate RFC 9309 matching and failure behavior plus bounded same-authority sitemap discovery; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sitemap Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-sitemap-boundaries-1rhn2o" that uses case 9859 to validate RFC 9309 matching and failure behavior plus bounded same-authority sitemap discovery. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-sitemap-boundaries-1rhn2o
# Competency: scraper-sitemap-boundaries.
# Case 9859: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_sitemap_boundaries_1rhn2o(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1rhn2o = "1rhn2o-o2nhr1-9859"
    assert evidence_variant_1rhn2o
    evidence_axes_1rhn2o = (
        "authorization1rhn2o",
        "url9859",
        "responseo2nhr1",
        "tree1rhn2o9859",
        "frontier98591rhn2o",
        "reporto2nhr19859",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1rhn2o)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"
